title: Add volumes-from support using annotation in kube yaml
body:
The reserved annotation io.podman.annotations.volumes-from is made public to let user define volumes-from to have one container mount volumes of other containers.

The annotation format is: io.podman.annotations.volumes-from/tgtCtr: "srcCtr1:mntOpts1;srcCtr2:mntOpts;..."

Fixes: containers#16819

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change? Yes

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Annotation io.podman.annotations.volumes-from can be used to define list of volumes-from to have one container mount volumes of other containers. The format of the annotation is io.podman.annotations.volumes-from/targetContainer: "sourceContainer1:mntOptions1;sourceContainer2:mntOptions;...".
```

LGTM. but the machine tests should be passing. Recommend a rebase to pick up latest main, might fix.

> LGTM. but the machine tests should be passing. Recommend a rebase to pick up latest main, might fix.

One of the machine tests continue to fail for an independent reason even after rebasing. Is there anything else that I can do or is it good to get merged?

@ygalblum ,
One clarifying question about your comment https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21510#discussion_r1486322336 _the length of the value of the annotation is limited to 253 characters_.

As per https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/#syntax-and-character-set, there are 63-char constraint for annotation name and 253-char for annotation prefix. Whereas, the annotation value can be up to 256KB.

Did you mean the same or is it that Podman has a constraint of 253-char for annotation value?

@vikas-goel thanks for pointing this out.
It seems that there was a misunderstanding of the limits of the length of the value of an annotations in Podman, limiting the value length to 63 unless `--no-trunc` is passed. I've opened #21667 to handle it
